class.name: BallisticBody3D
class.brief_description: A 3D physics body for projectiles that raycasts along its velocity to detect impacts.
class.description: A [RigidBody3D] designed for ballistic projectiles such as bullets, arrows, or other fast-moving objects. Every physics frame, it casts a ray from its previous position to its current position along its velocity vector, preventing fast projectiles from tunneling through objects. When a hit is detected, the node reads the [PhysicsMaterial] from the collider to determine the outcome: - If [member penetration_power] exceeds the material's [member PhysicsMaterial.penetration_resistance], the projectile penetrates through, losing speed proportionally, and the [signal penetrated] signal is emitted. - If the material's [member PhysicsMaterial.reflection_factor] is greater than zero and the projectile cannot penetrate, it ricochets off the surface and the [signal ricocheted] signal is emitted. - Otherwise, the projectile stops and freezes at the impact point, and the [signal hit] signal is emitted. If the collider's [PhysicsMaterial] has an [member PhysicsMaterial.impact_scene] assigned, it is instantiated at the hit point, oriented along the surface normal. This can be used for impact effects such as particles, decals, or sound. The node uses the inherited [member CollisionObject3D.collision_mask] to determine which objects it can hit, and always excludes itself from the raycast.

method.name: get_ballistic_state
method.description: Returns the current state of the projectile. See [enum BallisticState].

method.name: get_hit_normal
method.description: Returns the surface normal at the most recent impact point. Only valid after a hit, penetration, or ricochet has occurred.

method.name: get_hit_position
method.description: Returns the world-space position of the most recent impact. Only valid after a hit, penetration, or ricochet has occurred.

members.member: The penetrating force of this projectile. When the projectile hits a surface, this value is compared against the [PhysicsMaterial]'s [member PhysicsMaterial.penetration_resistance]. If [member penetration_power] is greater, the projectile passes through and its speed is reduced proportionally. Each penetration reduces the remaining [member penetration_power] by the material's resistance value.
Extra length added to the end of the raycast along the velocity direction. This helps catch impacts that occur just past the projectile's current position, preventing near-misses at high speeds.

signal.name: hit
signal.description: Emitted when the projectile impacts a surface and comes to a full stop. The projectile is frozen in place after this signal. [param position] is the world-space hit location and [param normal] is the surface normal.

signal.name: penetrated
signal.description: Emitted when the projectile penetrates through a surface and continues flying. This occurs when [member penetration_power] exceeds the material's [member PhysicsMaterial.penetration_resistance]. The projectile's speed is reduced after penetration.

signal.name: ricocheted
signal.description: Emitted when the projectile ricochets off a surface. This occurs when the material's [member PhysicsMaterial.reflection_factor] is greater than zero and the projectile cannot penetrate. The projectile's velocity is reflected along the surface normal, scaled by the reflection factor.

constants.constant: The projectile has hit a surface and stopped.
The projectile has penetrated through a surface. This is a transient state; the projectile returns to [constant STATE_FLYING] immediately after.
The projectile has ricocheted off a surface. This is a transient state; the projectile returns to [constant STATE_FLYING] immediately after.
